governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: Raw Claude host artifacts outside the OpenBox artifact evidence contract are host-owned.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox governs artifacts only through plugin, MCP, hook payload, and explicit SDK evidence fields that enter Core.

governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: Arbitrary Codex host crashes outside OpenBox-governed failure events are host-owned.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox records WorkflowFailed for Codex events and hook/runtime failures that pass through the official Codex integration path.

governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: Arbitrary Codex task or agent routing outside OpenBox MCP/tool handoff metadata is host-owned.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox records Handoff from explicit Codex MCP, tool, and a2a metadata fields owned by the integration contract.

governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: Raw Cursor host artifacts outside the OpenBox artifact evidence contract are host-owned.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox governs artifacts only through Cursor hook payload, MCP, plugin asset, and explicit SDK evidence fields that enter Core.

governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: Cursor outputs with no native safe transform shape cannot be mutated by OpenBox.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox enforces constrain on Cursor outputs with a supported mutation shape and fails closed for unsupported transform shapes.

governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: Generic OpenAI Agents signal events outside OpenBox run goal or signal input are not OpenBox-controlled.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox records SignalReceived from run-start, goal, and explicit signal fields in the OpenBox Agents SDK wrapper.

governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: Arbitrary caller handoffs outside explicit OpenBox MCP handoff arguments are caller-owned.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox records Handoff from check_governance and reporting tool arguments that carry explicit handoff metadata.

governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: MCP goal alignment requires caller-provided OpenBox goal context.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox runs goal alignment for MCP calls that use the required goal context fields and fails closed where strict goal context is required but unresolved.

governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: Arbitrary CopilotKit or a2a routing outside explicit AG-UI/OpenBox handoff metadata is host-owned.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox records Handoff from governed CopilotKit runtime, tool, and AG-UI metadata fields.

governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: Arbitrary n8n workflow crashes outside the OpenBox n8n failure helper or template metadata are host-owned.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox records WorkflowFailed for n8n helper, node, and template failure events that enter the OpenBox runtime contract.

governanceChecklistLimitations.limitation: Arbitrary n8n routing outside OpenBox n8n workflow or AI Agent handoff metadata is host-owned.
governanceChecklistLimitations.openboxContract: OpenBox records Handoff from generated n8n workflow, AI Agent, node, and helper metadata fields.

surfaces.notes: Generated from TypeSpec and installed by the Claude Code plugin.
OpenBox skill ships under plugin skills/openbox.
Compatibility command markdown files remain for Claude slash entrypoints.
OpenBox reviewer agent ships in the plugin.
CLAUDE/AGENTS-style governance instructions are rendered from the shared OpenBox rules projection renderer.
OpenBox MCP server exposes status, doctor, approvals, agents, rules, policies, and governance checks.
Only agent/subagentStatusLine are currently supported by Claude Code plugin settings.
Documented as opt-in because monitors run unsandboxed and project-scope plugins do not load them.
No OpenBox language server exists; official LSP plugins should be installed separately.
Plugin ships a project-local Node runner for hooks, MCP, and diagnostics; it resolves a project-local SDK package.
Enterprise policy belongs to managed Claude Code deployment, not SDK mutation.
Research preview MCP push channel surface; standard MCP remains the connector path.
PreToolUse/PermissionRequest routing covers current built-in tool names and dynamic mcp__ tools.

sdkCapabilities.coverage: SessionStart opens the workflow and records the Claude session boundary.
Stop completes workflows with no background tasks; SessionEnd remains opt-in shutdown telemetry.
StopFailure emits observe telemetry and then records WorkflowFailed best-effort.
PreToolUse opens a stable activity and PostToolUse/PostToolUseFailure closes it with output/duration.
Prompts, permission requests, compaction, config changes, tasks, final output, subagents, and MCP elicitation map to activity gates.
UserPromptSubmit emits the generated goal signal for AGE goal capture before the prompt gate; assistant-output spans are emitted only on completed assistant output.
Claude hook rendering supports remote polling, inline ask, defer, and fail-closed deny/block shapes for decision-capable hooks.
Claude verdict renderer preserves allow+updatedInput, additionalContext, updatedToolOutput, and elicitation accept content where Claude supports mutation.
Decision-capable hooks return Claude-native block/deny/continue=false responses and mark halted sessions for later hooks.
Prompt, shell, file, HTTP, and MCP tool paths attach spans so behavior rules can match the same shapes used by other SDK adapters.
Plugin .mcp.json points at the bundled project-local Node runner for mcp serve; MCP exposes status, doctor, approvals, agent/rule/policy reads, and check_governance.
Export/install packages skill, commands, agent, hooks, MCP, diagnostics, project-local bin runner/doctor shim, and explicit settings/monitor/LSP inventory.
Claude hooks read official project-local settings env plus OpenBox-owned project state only; old hook-runtime directories are not consulted.
Claude Code does not embed CopilotKit UI wrappers; it maps the same governance primitives through hooks and MCP instead.

publicIntegrationSupport.notes: Native helpers plus compatibility wrappers for the OpenAI Agents SDK.
Native hook/options/query wrapper coverage; WorktreeCreate remains opt-in at host level.
CopilotKit runtime, AG-UI event stream, React renderers, and headless HITL helpers.
Low-level primitives, semantic governance-check helper, packaged credentials, nodes, and workflow template descriptors.

guardrailCapabilityGuards.failClosedBehavior: Unsupported or failed redaction blocks or denies continuation instead of allowing the original payload through.

undrivableTriggers.reason: The current public SDK/host verdict-matrix API accepts LocalGovernanceSpanType=llm and computes llm_completion. Direct llm_gen_ai classification is still covered by the Core/OPA local-stack scenario matrix until the SDK exposes an explicit generation span type.

The verdict matrix drives MCP calls through the SDK/MCP span path and proves Core default-allow behavior. Backend behavior-rule seeding does not currently produce a rule-specific allow reason for this trigger, so rule-backed mcp_tool_call enforcement stays covered by the Core/OPA scenario matrix.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: OPA/Rego allows a governed event
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: The local stack must prove an OPA allow result through generated policy evaluation or Core governance evaluation.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: OPA/Rego requires approval
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A generated policy must return require_approval, create a pending approval, and expose the approval through Core polling.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: OPA/Rego blocks a governed event
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A generated policy must return block and Core must expose the blocking verdict without allowing the action.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: OPA/Rego halts a governed event
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A generated policy must return halt and Core must expose the halted verdict without allowing the action.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: OPA/Rego legacy decision aliases map to canonical verdicts
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Legacy OPA decision aliases must map through Core/backend to canonical verdicts: continue to allow, stop to halt, and require-approval to require_approval.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: OPA/Rego constrain contract boundary
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A generated CONSTRAIN policy must prove the real backend/Core boundary: backend accepts the policy and Core propagates the constrain verdict/action to SDK callers.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: OPA unavailable fails closed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: If OPA is unavailable, Core must fail closed with a halt/block class result rather than allow.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Approval pending state is materialized
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A require_approval verdict must appear in agent, organization, and Core polling surfaces as pending.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Approval approve decision resolves pending row
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: An approve decision must mutate the pending approval into approved history and allow the stored verdict.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Approval reject decision resolves pending row
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A reject decision must mutate the pending approval into rejected history and keep the action denied.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Approval timeout/expiration fails closed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Expired approvals must fail closed and must not be silently converted into allow.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Approval dashboard metrics, history, and SLA are shaped
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Approval dashboard metrics, history, and SLA endpoints must assert seeded pending and decided approval rows plus stable dashboard rollup shape.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Guardrail allows safe input/output
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: The local stack must prove safe guardrail input returns a successful run-test result with violations_detected=false.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Guardrail blocks unsafe input/output
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: The local stack must prove unsafe guardrail input is detected with violations_detected=true and transformed output rather than pass-through.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Guardrail redacts or transforms sensitive fields
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Redaction/constrain output must be asserted as returned transformed payload, not only endpoint reachability.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Guardrail service unavailable fails closed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Unavailable guardrail service paths must fail closed or be explicitly marked unavailable; they cannot count as behavioral proof.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Goal signal precedes first governed action
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: The suite must prove goal capture happens before the first governed prompt/tool/action.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Database query span is governed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A database query span must be classified and governed through the local stack.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Tool-call span is governed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A tool-call span must be classified and governed through the local stack.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: HTTP span is governed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: An HTTP span must be classified and governed through the local stack.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: File-read span is governed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A file-read span must be classified and governed through the local stack.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: File-write span is governed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A file-write span must be classified and governed through the local stack.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Shell span is governed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A shell/internal command span must be classified and governed through the local stack.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: LLM span is governed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: An LLM generation/completion span must be classified and governed through the local stack.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: MCP tool span is governed
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: An MCP tool span must be classified and governed through the local stack or declared observe-only for library providers.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Behavior rule lifecycle persists current state
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Behavior-rule create, update, current-version, status toggle, and delete paths must assert persisted state, not only HTTP 200. The current-rule surface returns the current version and carries its is_active state.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Behavior rule metrics and violations are shaped
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Behavior-rule metrics and violation list endpoints must assert dashboard shape and a seeded behavior_violated row so behavioral failure coverage does not count as smoke coverage.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Behavior rule history and rollback are stateful
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Behavior-rule update must create a versioned history, and rollback must make the selected historical version current again.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Policy lifecycle, evaluations, and metrics are stateful
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Policy current/update/metrics/evaluation endpoints must assert persisted policy state and DB-backed evaluation rows.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Guardrail lifecycle, ordering, metrics, and logs are stateful
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Guardrail lifecycle, ordering, metrics, and violation-log endpoints must assert persisted state and DB-backed evaluation rows.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Core accepts usage/cost wire fields and materializes supported token metrics
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Core must accept every LocalStackUsageWireCaseId top-level usage/cost payload, including nonzero token/cost and explicit zero token/cost values, persist the governance event, materialize only supported nonzero token metric rows, and avoid fabricating total-token or cost metric rows.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Token usage is normalized by provider adapters
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Token aliases normalize through generated/provider SDK guards; /agent/metrics is a dashboard aggregate, not the local token ledger.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Explicit Cost USD telemetry is forwarded, not computed by adapters
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Cost aliases normalize through generated/provider SDK guards from explicit upstream telemetry or an OpenBox estimate; adapters never compute spend locally and Core remains policy authority.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Zero usage/cost values are preserved by provider adapters
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Explicit zero token and cost fields are preserved by SDK normalization tests; the local backend has no usage/cost write API to seed this path.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Backend dashboard metrics expose stable rollups
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Backend agent metrics are dashboard rollups and must assert agent/guardrail/policy sections rather than only HTTP 200.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: AIVSS and trust ledger surfaces are stateful
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: AIVSS update/recalculate and trust dashboard endpoints must assert DB-backed trust history, event, recovery, and org trend state.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Goal alignment check result is surfaced
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Core governance results must surface whether goal alignment was checked.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Goal drift detection is surfaced
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: The suite must prove a drifted result through the goal-drift endpoints.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: AGE unavailable marks governance checks incomplete
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: With AGE unavailable, Core must set governance_checks_incomplete under an isolated AGE-unavailable local-stack run.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Local LlamaFirewall official scanner returns aligned and drifted decisions
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: The local stack must run the official LlamaFirewall scanner adapter against a structured-output local model and prove both aligned and drifted traces without schema downgrade.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Session trace surfaces are readable
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Session list/detail endpoints must assert returned state, not only status.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Governance/log trace surfaces are readable
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Log and governance feed endpoints must assert returned trace/log content.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Reasoning trace surface is readable
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Reasoning trace endpoints must assert returned trace content.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Provider/source attribution is retained
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Governed events must retain provider/source attribution through the persisted _openbox_source input field; provider span stamping is covered by adapter guard tests because the local Core path does not persist submitted openbox.source span attributes on governance_events.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Observability dashboard and logs read seeded ledger state
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Observability, issues, insights, and agent logs must assert seeded local-stack telemetry rows and dashboard rollup shapes.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Violation ledgers support false-positive marking
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Violation list endpoints must expose seeded violation rows and false-positive marking must persist the is_false_positive state.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Workflow session lifecycle is readable
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: A seeded local-stack workflow session must be visible in list/detail/log/reasoning/stat surfaces with asserted state.

localStackScenarioPaths.label: Workflow session termination changes active state
localStackScenarioPaths.requiredBehavior: Terminating an active workflow session must remove it from active sessions and persist halted detail state.

localStackScenarioMatrix.description: Backend/Core governance conformance matrix for OPA/Rego, approvals, guardrails, behavior rules, goal drift, tracing/span, usage/cost, workflow ordering, and DB-backed dashboard paths. Provider-owned usage/cost normalization is allowed only when declared outside local-stack runtime proof.

requiredObjectiveSpecs.label: Backend usage, cost, and trust operations

localStackScenarioMatrix.providerGuardSharedProofPolicy: Shared provider guard proof is allowed only for explicitly generated backend-owned capabilities. OPA/Rego and behavior-rule evaluation are backend-owned, so every SDK/runtime provider must prove it delegates that capability rather than implementing policy evaluation locally.
localStackScenarioMatrix.localStackAxisPolicy: Every required governance axis, including approval, Rego, and span handling, must be represented by at least one proven local-stack scenario; provider-owned fixture evidence cannot satisfy the local-stack governance axis requirement.
localStackScenarioMatrix.rawSemanticGapPolicy: Raw backend/Core semantic gaps must remain explicit in the local-stack ledger and each gap must have generated TypeScript and Python request-preflight closure evidence until backend/Core rejects the invalid input itself.
localStackScenarioMatrix.backendCoreGapStatusPolicy: backendCoreGapStatus is gap-free when no raw backend/Core semantic gaps remain; while raw gaps exist, it must remain known-gaps until the raw local stack rejects those inputs directly.
localStackScenarioMatrix.backendCoreGapRemediationPolicy: Every raw backend/Core semantic gap must map to a generated request constraint remediation target with the affected operations, constraint keys, raw proof file, and required raw rejection behavior.
